stem: A SaaS analytics study surveys 15 subscribers and observes 3 positive outcomes out of 15 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (9 observations, rate = 11.1%) and Group B (6 observations, rate = 33.3%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 33% and 51% of the population. Estimate the true customer churn rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.2609 (range [0.2454, 0.2764]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0705, 0.4519]. Point estimate: 0.2609.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.42: 0.42×0.161 + 0.58×0.3333 = 0.2609.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
